channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Donald Trump vows to help ‘troubled’ Hollywood with Mel Gibson, Jon Voight and Sylvester Stallone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump wants to make Hollywood “bigger, better and stronger” and has cast Mel Gibson, Jon Voight and Sylvester Stallone as stars of what he is calling his “Special Ambassadors to a great but very troubled place, Hollywood, California.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden's pointed warning about the U.S. becoming an “ oligarchy ” of tech billionaires will be illustrated at Donald Trump's inauguration, when the world's three richest men will sit on the dais as Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Driver in Texas migrant smuggling run that led to the deaths of 53 people pleads guilty
item.description: A Texas truck driver charged in the deaths of 53 migrants inside a sweltering tractor-trailer with no air conditioning has pleaded guilty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nazi flag-bearing man gets 8 years in prison for truck crashing near White House
item.description: A Missouri man who crashed a rental truck into barriers protecting the White House has been sentenced to eight years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Missing Indianapolis woman found safe: Police
item.description: Detectives believed McGann is in need of medical attention and asked if located, please call 911 immediately.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows Santa Fe man accused of spending the night inside a Walmart
item.description: After searching the store, lapel footage shows police finding a man they identified as Rodolpho Mendiola in the pet supplies aisle.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jeff Bezos’ New Glenn rocket reaches orbit on first test flight
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Blue Origin launched its massive new rocket on its first test flight Thursday, sending up a prototype satellite to orbit thousands of miles above Earth.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fire at home of Florida man missing for 2 years could offer investigators clues
item.description: A recent fire destroyed the home of a Florida man who has been missing for nearly two years, deepening the mystery surrounding his disappearance and reigniting the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Jewish students ace Abraham Accords 101
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sub-freezing temperatures, sunshine forecast for Trump inauguration
item.description: Sub-freezing temperatures and sunshine are forecast for President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: He left his LA-area home to cover the wildfires. But the flames were barreling toward his front door
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — The warnings began on Saturday, Jan. 4. Our neighbor, a volunteer at the local sheriff's station, texted that we should “batten down the hatches” for a “big windstorm." From there, things moved fast.
item.dc:creator: RYAN PEARSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jack Hoffman, the young cancer patient who ran for a famous 2013 touchdown, dies at 19
item.description: Jack Hoffman, the cancer patient who captured the hearts of Nebraska football fans and inspired the nation as a 7-year-old when he ran for a touchdown during the Cornhuskers' 2013 spring game, has died at the age of 19.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, Fox 5

item.title: Small California town considers backing mass deportations
item.description: "The way the language was presented in the resolution, it suggests that people should be scared of immigrants because they are criminals," said Rios. "It would target them by other members of the community who believe immigrants are causing harm and need to be sanctioned by the city of El Cajon.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Harbor seal, flamingo at Chicago zoo die from bird flu
item.description: A Harbor Seal and Chilean Flamingo at the Lincoln Park Zoo died as a result of avian influenza last week.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance's Ohio hometown struggles with how to recognize the incoming vice president
item.description: MIDDLETOWN, Ohio (AP) — It was a month after her son's election as vice president of the United States and JD Vance 's mother wondered why the city of his birth had yet to recognize him.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: FDA proposes sweeping limits on nicotine in cigarettes
item.description: It's an 11th-hour plan from the Biden administration that will take years to go into effect, if it happens at all.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: US government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 Grammys to proceed with focus on wildfire relief. Here's what you can expect
item.description: The 2025 Grammy Awards are right around the corner, which means it is time to get those viewing party plans in action. Allow us to help.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Netanyahu says Cabinet won't meet over ceasefire until Hamas drops new demands
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down from what it called a “last minute crisis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alabama inmate asks court to block nitrogen gas execution
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Attorneys for an Alabama inmate scheduled to be the fourth person put to death with nitrogen gas on Wednesday asked a federal judge to block the execution, arguing that the first three inmates showed signs of suffering from suffocation as the gas flowed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coors Light is temporarily changing its name: Here's why
item.description: This year, Coors Light is turning the case of the Mondays into a literal case, with limited-edition packaging and designs.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KRON4

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US in plea talks with Mexican cartel kingpin Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. prosecutors said Wednesday that they're discussing a potential plea deal with Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, the long-elusive Mexican drug lord who was arrested last summer and whose son could testify against him if he goes to trial. Assistant U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US population projections shrink from last year because of declining birth rates, less immigration
item.description: The Congressional Budget Office has shrunk its projections for the U.S. population in 30 years to 372 million residents, a 2.8% drop from last year, citing declining birth rates and less expected immigration.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Craft retailer Joann files for bankruptcy
item.description: Craft retailer Joann filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on Wednesday morning, marking the second time the company has done so in a year.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Chefs Cook initiative aims to help culinary community impacted by Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Chefs and restaurateurs across Chicagoland have stepped up to help by pledging a portion of proceeds from special menu items to the cause.
item.dc:creator: Lourdes Duarte, WGN 9

item.title: Octavia Butler imagined LA ravaged by fires. Her Altadena cemetery survived
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — Decades ago, the writer Octavia Butler had imagined a Los Angeles ravaged by fires. The Altadena cemetery where the science fiction and Afrofuturism author is buried did catch fire last week but suffered “minimal damage,” according to a statement on the cemetery's web site.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and CHRIS PIZZELLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sexual predator sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah, planning to meet minor
item.description: A Florida man has been sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah and planning to have sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
